title: ExecutionGate
description: Hard execution gate derived from active-blocker.json. Prevents parallel work by constraining allowed actions. Checked BEFORE any task dispatch, file write, or git commit.

owner.description: Lane that owns this blocker — only this lane may work on it

unlocked_at.description: When the blocker was resolved (null while active)

description.description: Human-readable description of what this blocker controls

allowed_actions.description: Whitelist of action categories permitted while blocker is active. Any action NOT in this list is FORBIDDEN.

pattern.description: Glob or regex pattern matching forbidden file paths or actions

reason.description: Why this is forbidden under current blocker

forbidden.description: Explicit forbidden patterns — any match means the action is rejected

verification.description: How to verify the blocker is resolved

expected.description: Expected output or condition for resolution

_enforcement.description: This schema is enforced by scripts/execution-gate.js BEFORE any agent action
protocol: 1. Agent reads lanes/broadcast/active-blocker.json at session start
2. If blocker exists and status=active, agent loads execution-gate-v1.json
3. Before ANY write/commit/dispatch, agent validates action against allowed_actions + forbidden
4. If action is forbidden: STOP. Log violation. Do NOT proceed.
5. Only owner lane may modify files related to the blocker
7. On resolution: set status=resolved, unlocked_at=now, remove blocker within 60s

violation_handling.description: When an agent violates the gate
actions: Quarantine offending lane's write capability until review
